fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Massachusetts Audiologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a audiologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Massachusetts - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: audiologist license reciprocity massachusetts, how to transfer audiologist license to massachusetts, audiologist compact massachusetts, massachusetts audiologist endorsement application

board.name: Board of Registration of Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology

quick_facts.exam_name: Audiology Examination of the Praxis Series administered by the Educational Testing Service

license_types.title: AuD, CCC-A
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a audiologist in Massachusetts.

steps.title: Register and Apply Online
steps.description: Create an account on the Massachusetts ePLACE online licensing portal (eplace.mass.gov), then log in and initiate a 'New License Application' for an Audiologist license.

steps.title: Complete Application and Pay Fee
steps.description: Fill out all required sections of the Audiologist license application within ePLACE and pay the required application fee through the portal.

steps.title: Submit Academic Transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for official academic transcripts verifying your master's degree or its equivalent in audiology from an accredited academic institution to be submitted to the Board, typically by uploading them to ePLACE or having your institution send them electronically.

steps.title: Provide Verification of Experience
steps.description: Upload documentation verifying satisfactory completion of at least 400 hours of supervised clinical practicum and a minimum of 9 months (1260 hours) of supervised professional experience, often referred to as the Clinical Fellowship Year (CFY), to your ePLACE application.

steps.title: Submit Exam Scores
steps.description: Provide official documentation of a passing score on the National Praxis Examination in Audiology, usually by submitting the score report directly to the Board through ePLACE.

steps.title: Monitor Application Status
steps.description: Regularly check the status of your application within your ePLACE account for any requests for additional information or updates from the Board of Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the Praxis. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Massachusetts audiologist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Massachusetts have reciprocity for audiologists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers audiologist practice in Massachusetts. To work in Massachusetts, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my audiologist license to Massachusetts?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Massachusetts while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Audiologist in Massachusetts
hero.dek: To apply for an Audiology license in Massachusetts, you must submit your application and all supporting documentation through the state's online licensing portal, ePLACE.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps:
1. Go to the ePLACE portal (eplace.mass.gov) and click 'New Users: Register for an Account'.
2. Complete the registration form with your personal details, email, and create a password.
3. Verify your email address by clicking the link sent to your inbox.
4. Log in to ePLACE using your new credentials and select 'New License Application', then choose 'Audiologist'.
